row087_contract.title: Optical flow, velocity, acceleration, scuff, and force-cue extraction
row087_contract.completion_criteria: Camera-compensated motion and force proxies pass calibrated trajectory tests and remain labeled estimates.
required_acceptance: force cues labeled as estimates rather than physical ground truth
camera motion cannot become false actor motion

source_authority.authority: Row087 optical-flow, actor-relative motion, force-estimate, and camera-compensation requirements
trajectory, velocity-change, landmark/mask/depth, and contact-persistence fusion
force, attack, motion, contact, and event-identity audio boundary
frame/sample timing, motion window, contact, occlusion, and continuity QA

dependency_authority.row087_effect: Velocity and acceleration require an accepted frame, PTS, duration, cut, gap, and sample-clock authority.
Actor-relative and region-relative motion require accepted persistent owner tracks and masks.
Joint trajectories, gait/contact phases, and confidence are unavailable as accepted inputs to force-cue inference.

proven_capabilities: deterministic single-thread Farneback dense optical flow
flow median and p95 normalized by image diagonal
repeated PNG and JPEG byte determinism
tampered frame and nonfinite threshold fail-closed behavior
explicit_limitations: planned camera motion is unsupported and fails closed
global phase translation is not a general camera-motion model
flow is full-frame rather than actor, object, body-region, or fabric scoped
no velocity, acceleration, force, sliding, scuffing, or fabric classification
no identity, body, hand, contact, audio, generation, visual-acceptance, or promotion claim

covered: repeated PNG and JPEG byte determinism
not_covered: planned pan, tilt, zoom, rotation, perspective, parallax, or rolling-shutter compensation
velocity, acceleration, force, sliding, scuffing, or fabric cues

adjacent_retained_runtime_metric.row087_interpretation: This is genuine retained execution evidence for the adjacent analyzer. It directly proves that current camera handling is insufficient for Row087 rather than proving camera-compensated actor motion.

row087_non_authority_reasons: The evidence belongs to Row021 and is a bounded temporal-quality review.
It does not bind actor-relative flow or accepted Row085 tracks.
It does not compute velocity, acceleration, force, sliding, scuffing, or fabric events.
It explicitly withholds fine hand, foot, toe, and production video-lane certification.

useful_shape: primary, micro, secondary, contact, camera, and stabilization classes
contact graph, material profiles, gravity, units, and solver policy
missing_or_underconstrained_authority: no optical-flow artifact or vector-field binding
no force-proxy value, units, uncertainty, or explicit estimate label
no sliding, scuffing, or fabric-motion classification
no calibration set, algorithm, metric, threshold, or benchmark
no semantic ordering invariant for PTS, frame, or sample spans
gravity vector permits more than three elements
zero amplitude and frequency limits are permitted without semantic qualification

adversarial_motion_physics_schema_probe.command_family: python jsonschema Draft202012Validator over the third-pass schema registry
adversarial_motion_physics_schema_probe.interpretation: The closed planning schema accepts reversed spans, a four-component gravity vector, and zero limits while rejecting direct fields required to record Row087 measurements and estimate authority.

adjacent_wave28_micro_motion_scaffold.assessment: The compiler copies caller-supplied motion events and the scorer averages caller-supplied scores. These are routing and scoring scaffolds, not extraction, calibration, or Row087 runtime authority.

downstream_force_alignment_partial_autho.focused_command: python -m pytest -q Plan/Instructions/QA/Scripts/test_wave64_foley_force_alignment_strict.py -k 'low_confidence_fails or frame_drift_fails or duplicate_force_ids_invalid or missing_runtime_blocked'
downstream_force_alignment_partial_autho.row087_non_authority_reason: The evaluator consumes a precomputed force-event manifest and validates downstream AV alignment. It does not extract or calibrate motion and force cues from video.

required_motion_force_contract_missing: accepted Row084 timeline, Row085 owner tracks, and Row086 landmarks and phases
content-addressed optical-flow field per frame pair
owner, object, body-region, clothing, surface, and background masks
camera model covering translation, rotation, zoom, perspective, parallax, and cut boundaries
camera transform and residual flow after compensation
local, world, actor-relative, object-relative, and surface-relative coordinate definitions
velocity, deceleration, and acceleration algorithms with units and uncertainty
approach, impact, slide, scuff, friction, and fabric-motion cue classifiers
force-proxy definition explicitly labeled estimated and nonphysical
calibration dataset, annotation, metrics, thresholds, and confidence reliability
low-confidence, unknown, occluded, and unsupported-motion certification ceiling
deterministic replay and tamper-resistant extraction receipt
combined flow overlay, track, contact, cue, and audio visual-review binding
acceptance_matrix_required: static camera and static scene yield near-zero compensated actor motion
pure pan, tilt, zoom, rotation, and perspective motion do not become actor motion
foreground actor motion remains after camera compensation
multiple actors retain separate relative trajectories
approach velocity and deceleration match annotated trajectories
impact acceleration proxy peaks at annotated contact without claiming physical force
sliding and scuffing separate from stepping and camera motion
fabric motion separates from body, background, and illumination motion
occlusion and reappearance preserve uncertainty and owner identity
cuts, duplicated frames, dropped frames, and VFR intervals fail closed
depth and scale changes do not silently corrupt pixel-to-world interpretation
low-texture, blur, flicker, and illumination changes expose low confidence
calibrated errors and confidence remain within registered per-cue thresholds
deterministic replay preserves fields, transforms, cues, metrics, and hashes
tampered frames, tracks, camera state, thresholds, or receipts are rejected
combined frame, flow, track, contact, cue, and audio review passes on genuine media

test_and_runtime_boundary.ec2_not_invoked_reason: Three prerequisite rows, planned-camera compensation, owner-relative decomposition, force-estimate semantics, calibration, and direct evidence already fail locally; GPU execution cannot establish those missing authorities.

scheduler_and_worker_containment.containment_note: The signed intent was deferred with no routing or wake. Existing paused automations, disabled worker tasks, disabled Mask maintenance tasks, worker snapshots, FLUX2 paths, portfolio paths, and unrelated dirty files were not modified.

hold_decision.reason: Rows084, 085, and 086 remain incomplete. The project has a strong deterministic Wave27 Farneback-flow and static-camera analyzer, registered thresholds, ten passing analyzer tests, a retained eight-frame runtime metric, bounded Row021 visual evidence, a draft motion/physics plan, and four passing downstream force-alignment tests. The retained runtime directly fails planned-camera support. No production Row087 extractor computes owner-relative motion, velocity, acceleration, force estimates, sliding, scuffing, fabric cues, or general camera compensation; no calibrated trajectory benchmark, estimate/uncertainty contract, direct runtime receipt, combined review, or tracker output exists.
hold_decision.safe_next_action: After Rows084-086 are accepted, freeze a Row087 measurement contract binding canonical time, owner tracks, landmarks/phases, dense flow fields, camera transforms, coordinate spaces, velocity/acceleration units, cue classifiers, explicit nonphysical force estimates, uncertainty, calibration fixtures, metrics, thresholds, and hashes. Extend the deterministic analyzer from static translation to planned camera transforms and owner-mask residuals; validate static, pan, tilt, zoom, rotation, parallax, crossing actors, occlusion, cuts/gaps, sliding, scuffing, fabric, low texture, blur, flicker, tamper, and replay cases; then run genuine combined flow/track/contact/audio review before emitting the tracker-declared evidence. Continue independent Codex PM/QA work meanwhile.
